title: Coffee Ritual
text: Most Italians drink espresso standing at the bar.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: In Naples, coffee is served zuccherato — sweetened before it reaches your hand.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: A caffè macchiato is espresso 'stained' with a drop of milk.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Greeks call their strong brew ellinikós kafés — boiled slowly in a briki.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Turkish coffee is grounds, water, and time — never rushed.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: In Lisbon, a bica at the counter costs less than sitting down.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: The afternoon espresso is sacred — never after dinner in Italy.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Cappuccino belongs to the morning. The barista will forgive a tourist once.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Spanish café con leche pairs with a simple tostada at dawn.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Moroccan cafés serve thé à la menthe — but espresso has found its way in.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: The ritual begins with the hiss of the machine, not the first sip.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: In Sicily, a coffee granita in summer is breakfast and dessert.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: French Riviera cafés open before the sun hits the harbour.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Two sugars, one conversation — the bar is a social club.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Croatian coastal towns adopted espresso from Venetian traders centuries ago.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: The perfect crema should hold a spoonful of sugar for a second.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Order 'un caffè' — never 'espresso' if you want to sound local.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Third-wave roasters now sit beside century-old bars in every port city.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: A morning coffee in the piazza sets the pace for the entire day.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: In Greece, 'ena kafe parakalo' opens almost any conversation.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: The barista remembers your order before you reach the counter.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Decaf exists, but the Mediterranean pretends not to notice.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Corretto means 'corrected' — espresso with a splash of grappa or sambuca.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Shade, ceramic cup, and five minutes of stillness — that is the ritual.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Maltese kafè is strong, dark, and best beside the Grand Harbour.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: The first espresso of the day tastes different in a town you've never visited.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: In Barcelona, café solo arrives in a glass on the hottest afternoons.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Never drink coffee on the run — the Mediterranean considers it rude to the coffee.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: A well-run bar pulls hundreds of espressos before lunch without a word wasted.

title: Coffee Ritual
text: Coffee here is fuel, greeting, and punctuation between stories.
